name: Alabama River Cellulose Mill
products: Fluff pulp, market softwood pulp — diapers, tissue, food packaging
capacity_notes: ~1M tpy after $800M expansion (2027 target); current ~900kt; largest US softwood pulp mill
notes: Acquired from Parsons & Whittemore 2010. $1.5B+ total GP investment. $800M modernization 2025-2027 adds 300 tpd. New digester, pulp dryer, power boiler. World's #1 GP Cellulose market share ~28% (2015 RISI).
source: GP press releases 2024-2025; Made in Alabama 2025; BusinessAlabama 2018

name: Brunswick Cellulose Mill
products: Fluff pulp, bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: Est ~350kt fluff + market pulp grades
notes: Port of Brunswick access for export. One of GP Cellulose's original mills.
source: GP Cellulose website; RISI data references

name: Leaf River Cellulose Mill
products: Fluff pulp, bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: Est ~400kt fluff + market pulp
notes: Leaf River (Perry County). One of GP Cellulose's core mills.

name: Riegelwood Mill
products: Fluff pulp, southern bleached softwood kraft — diapers, feminine hygiene
capacity_notes: One machine operating (~700kt) after #20 machine permanently closed end-2023. Originally 1.4M tpy at peak (3 machines). Forecasted production increase 2026.
notes: $135M conversion to 100% fluff/softwood 2015-2016. #20 machine closed Oct 2023 (cut 300kt fluff). Remaining machine continues. Near Port of Wilmington NC for export. IP-Smurfit WestRock merger closed Jun 2024.
source: IP press releases 2015, 2023; FisherSolve/ResourceWise 2025; WECT news

name: Pensacola Mill
products: Fluff pulp, southern bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: Reduced after #4 machine permanently closed 2023. Remaining lines ~300kt.
notes: #4 machine idled Aug 2023, permanently closed Oct 2023 (cut 200kt SBSK). Remaining lines continue containerboard + pulp. Near Port Pensacola.
source: IP press release Oct 2023; Packaging Dive 2023

name: Columbus Cellulose Mill
products: Fluff pulp, specialty cellulose, modified fiber
capacity_notes: ~450kt pulp + modified fiber. Acquired from Weyerhaeuser 2016.
notes: Part of Weyerhaeuser Cellulose Fibers acquisition (5 mills, $2.2B, 2016). Modified fiber facility on same campus.

name: Flint River Mill
products: Fluff pulp, bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: ~380kt. Acquired from Weyerhaeuser 2016.
notes: Former Weyerhaeuser. Flint River provides process water.

name: Port Wentworth Mill
products: Fluff pulp — export-focused via Savannah port
capacity_notes: ~280kt. Port adjacent — major export fluff mill.
notes: Adjacent to Port of Savannah — major export terminal for fluff pulp to global markets. Acquired from Weyerhaeuser 2016.

name: New Bern Mill
products: Fluff pulp, bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: ~300kt. Acquired from Weyerhaeuser 2016.
notes: Neuse River. Former Weyerhaeuser. Near Port of Morehead City.

name: Georgetown Mill
products: Fluff pulp — WAS specialized fluff producer
capacity_notes: CLOSED late 2024. Was ~350kt/yr fluff (~5% US supply). FisherSolve Viability Index flagged as high-risk before closure.
notes: Closed late 2024 as part of IP cost optimization. ~5% of US fluff capacity removed. Winyah Bay/Sampit River port access. Strategic closure tightened global fluff supply-demand balance.

name: Franklin Mill
products: Fluff pulp, bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: ~400kt fluff + softwood. One of IP's original fluff mills.
notes: Blackwater River. Historic IP facility. Near Port of Virginia (Norfolk/Newport News).

name: Naheola Mill
products: Angel Soft, Quilted Northern bath tissue; Brawny, Sparkle towels; Dixie plate/cup stock (SBS paperboard)
capacity_notes: ~300kt tissue/towel + SBS paperboard. New $120M tissue machine (replaced 2 older) startup 2020. Biomass boiler + woodyard modernized.
notes: $500M+ invested past 5 yrs. New tissue converting lines. Also produces bleached paperboard for Dixie cups/plates. Near Tombigbee River.
source: GP press releases 2017-2020; Thompson Engineering; Nonwovens Industry

name: Broadway Mill
products: Brawny paper towels, Quilted Northern bath tissue, private label — TAD technology
capacity_notes: ~250kt+ after $550M TAD machine expansion (2024 startup). 7 paper machines + converting.
notes: Larger of 2 GP Green Bay plants. $550M Brawny TAD project completed 2024 — new through-air-dried machine for premium towels. Fox River corridor — historic papermaking region. Day Street facility closed.
source: GP History; IEN 2023; Green Bay Press-Gazette

name: Palatka Mill
products: Angel Soft, Brawny, Sparkle, private label tissue/towels; kraft paper (mailers, grocery bags); linerboard
capacity_notes: ~350kt tissue/towel + kraft + linerboard. $400M TAD investment (2019 startup). 580-acre site on 6,000-acre campus.
notes: Largest private employer Putnam County. $1.2B+ GP investment since 2002. $83M warehouse expansion 2025. Produces tissue, towels, kraft paper, linerboard — highly diversified.
source: JAXUSA 2024; GP PR 2017; Jax Daily Record 2025

name: Halsey Mill
products: Quilted Northern Ultra Plush bath tissue, private label tissue
capacity_notes: ~120kt. $150M+ investment for capacity increase (announced Dec 2023). One of 2 GP pulp-to-tissue plants.
notes: Integrated pulp-to-tissue. North of Eugene. $200M+ total invested since 2022. Produces own pulp on site.

name: Muskogee Mill
products: Toilet paper, paper towels, napkins — consumer + commercial
capacity_notes: ~150kt tissue/towel. Integrated pulp-to-tissue (one of 2 GP plants).
notes: Arkansas River access. Produces pulp on site for tissue converting.

name: Mehoopany Plant
products: Charmin toilet paper, Bounty paper towels, Bounty napkins, Pampers diapers, Luvs diapers
capacity_notes: P&G's LARGEST US manufacturing site. Continuous roof >0.5 mile. Est 400kt+ tissue/towel + diaper output.
panjiva_consignee_names: PROCTER & GAMBLE, PROCTER AND GAMBLE, P&G MEHOOPANY
notes: Largest single source of Charmin in US. Susquehanna River. Major fluff pulp consumer for Pampers/Luvs diaper lines. Imports fluff pulp from Brazil (Suzano), Scandinavia, and domestic IP/GP mills.
source: P&G Locations; CLUI; Endless Mountains Visitors Bureau

name: Cape Girardeau Plant
products: Bounty paper towels, Charmin toilet paper, Pampers diapers, Luvs diapers
capacity_notes: 3M+ sqft, one of largest P&G US plants. 1,300-acre site.
notes: Began as Pampers diaper plant 1969, expanded to tissue/towel. Mississippi River access. Trail of Tears State Park adjacent.

name: Green Bay Plant
products: Bounty paper towels, Charmin toilet paper, Puffs facial tissue
capacity_notes: ~200kt tissue/towel. Fox River papermaking corridor.
notes: Former Charmin Paper Company site — P&G acquired Charmin 1957. Near GP Broadway mill. Fox River between Lake Winnebago and Lake Michigan.

name: Albany Plant
products: Bounty paper towels, Charmin toilet paper
capacity_notes: ~200kt. ~3M sqft. Southern Georgia.
notes: Major P&G tissue plant in southern GA. Serves Southeast distribution.

name: Oxnard Plant
products: Charmin toilet paper, Bounty paper towels
capacity_notes: ~150kt. Built by International Paper 1969, acquired P&G 1973.
notes: One of 2 large tissue plants in SoCal (other is K-C Fullerton). Serves West Coast.

name: Box Elder Plant
products: Charmin toilet paper, Bounty paper towels
capacity_notes: ~80-120kt. $540M investment. P&G's first greenfield in US in 30 years (opened ~2010-2011).
notes: Near Walmart Distribution Center — Walmart is #1 retailer of Charmin. Serves western/intermountain region. NW of Brigham City.

name: Beech Island Plant
products: Huggies diapers, Pull-Ups, Cottonelle, Scott bath tissue, Kleenex facial tissue, Viva towels
capacity_notes: K-C's LARGEST US production plant. ~2,000 employees. $200M automated distribution center under construction (2025-2027).
notes: K-C's largest single plant. Major fluff pulp consumer for Huggies/Pull-Ups. Cross-border from Augusta GA. $200M DC expansion + $2B+ company-wide US investment plan (2025). 18 mfg facilities + 39 DCs in NA.
source: K-C/Manufacturing Dive 2025; SC Future Makers; Post & Courier

name: Paris Plant
products: Huggies diapers, Pull-Ups, Little Swimmers, GoodNites training pants
capacity_notes: Baby/child care focused. Opened May 1983. Celebrated 40th anniversary 2023.
notes: Dedicated infant/child care products. Major fluff pulp consumer. 100% green power (energy from landfill gas). Cathode mostly domestic (AZ) but imports fluff pulp from Brazil (Suzano Eucafluff).

name: Corinth Plant
products: Nonwoven fabrics — spunbond, meltblown, SMS; hygiene product components
capacity_notes: Nonwovens manufacturing. ISO certified.
notes: Nonwovens for Huggies, Depend, other K-C products. Processes: spunbond, meltblown, SMS, BCW, hydroentangled, film lamination, elastic lamination, airlaid, Coform.

name: Mobile Plant
products: Hygiene products — $130M new production line announced 2024
capacity_notes: $130M investment for new hygiene production line.
notes: $130M investment announced per AF&PA. Port of Mobile access.

name: Warren Plant (New)
products: Advanced manufacturing — K-C personal care products
capacity_notes: $800M greenfield. 1.1M sqft. Youngstown suburb. Construction began May 2025, 2-3 yr build.
notes: Part of K-C's $2B+ US investment plan. Advanced manufacturing facility. Construction started May 2025.

name: Chester Plant (Delco)
products: Scott toilet paper, Cottonelle — integrated mill acquired via Scott Paper 1995
capacity_notes: ~120kt. One of older integrated mills from Scott Paper acquisition.
notes: Delaware River — port adjacent. One of K-C's older integrated mills from 1995 Scott Paper acquisition ($9.4B). Near Philadelphia.

name: Jenks/Owasso Plant
products: Scott, Cottonelle bath tissue; other disposable paper products
capacity_notes: ~100kt. South of Tulsa.
notes: Tulsa suburb. Multiple K-C tissue products.

name: Fullerton Plant
products: Scott, Cottonelle bath tissue
capacity_notes: ~80kt. Industrial zone. One of 2 large tissue plants in SoCal.
notes: Industrial warehouse zone. Near Port of Long Beach/LA — could receive imported pulp via port.

name: First Quality Tissue — Lock Haven
products: Ultra-premium private-label bath tissue, paper towels (store brands)
capacity_notes: ~200kt. 3 paper machines (3rd added 2019). 2.3M+ sqft (65 acres under roof). Former Hammermill/IP site.
notes: Former Hammermill/IP mill site purchased 2003 for $2M. Damaghi family (Iranian-American). Bald Eagle Creek water. 4th largest tissue producer in NA. Buys imported market pulp. #1 private-label ultra-premium tissue in US.
source: Lock Haven Express 2016, 2020, 2023; First Quality website

name: First Quality Tissue — Anderson
products: Private-label bath tissue, paper towels
capacity_notes: 3 tissue machines. 3.5M+ sqft campus. Former nylon fiber plant converted.
panjiva_consignee_names: FIRST QUALITY TISSUE, FIRST QUALITY ANDERSON
notes: Converted from idled nylon fiber plant. 3 TAD machines. Near Port of Charleston for imported pulp receipt.
source: Lock Haven Express 2016; First Quality website

name: First Quality Products — McElhattan
products: Prevail adult incontinence, baby diapers, baby wipes, feminine hygiene, disposable washcloths
capacity_notes: 600K sqft. Original FQ facility (1989). Major fluff pulp consumer.
panjiva_consignee_names: FIRST QUALITY PRODUCTS, FIRST QUALITY ENTERPRISES
notes: FQ's first facility. Adult incontinence (Prevail brand) + baby care + feminine hygiene. Imports fluff pulp from Brazil (Suzano) and domestic sources.
source: First Quality website; Lock Haven Express

name: First Quality Products — Macon
products: Baby diapers, adult incontinence, feminine care products
capacity_notes: 1.6M sqft. Major hygiene converting.
notes: Baby care + adult care. 50% capacity expansion announced 2024 (baby diapers +50%, adult care +25%).

name: First Quality Products — Lewistown
products: Nonwoven materials, converting operations
capacity_notes: 2.5M sqft. Largest FQ facility by sqft.
notes: Central PA. Nonwovens and hygiene product converting.

name: Essity Barton Mill
products: Tork paper towels, bath tissue, napkins — away-from-home (AFH)
capacity_notes: 1.2M sqft. ~120kt. Produces ~30% of Essity's NA Tork products per Harrodsburg site's claim. Buys 200k+ tons recycled paper/yr.
notes: Greenfield site built 2003. Cherokee/Shoals area. Makes Tork brand AFH products. 65% of recycled paper intake becomes finished product; residual used as farm nutrient supplement. Key AFH tissue supplier.

name: Essity Middletown Mill
products: Parent rolls (mother reels) of towel and tissue paper — feeds Harrodsburg converting
capacity_notes: ~80kt parent rolls. 150+ employees. Supports Harrodsburg KY converting.
notes: Produces parent rolls fed to Harrodsburg KY for Tork converting. New water-tube gas boiler for sustainability.
source: Warren County Post 2025; Tissue World Magazine 2023

name: Essity Harrodsburg Converting
products: Tork professional hygiene products — towels, bath tissue, napkins
capacity_notes: ~100kt converting. $220M expansion 2012. ~30% of Tork NA production.
panjiva_consignee_names: ESSITY, WAUSAU PAPER, SCA TISSUE HARRODSBURG
notes: Originally Bay West Paper Corp (1991), became Wausau-Mosinee, then Wausau Paper, acquired by Essity 2016. $220M expansion 2012. Central US distribution.

name: Sofidel Circleville Mill
products: Private-label bath tissue, paper towels for major US retailers
capacity_notes: Sofidel's largest, most modern US facility. 3rd TAD machine added Aug 2025. $500M+ total investment.
notes: Greenfield 2018. TAD technology. Supplies major grocery/supermarket chains' store-brand tissue. 4th largest tissue producer in NA. Sofidel = largest US private-label tissue producer.
source: Sofidel press releases; Wikipedia; LM Tribune 2024

name: Sofidel Inola Mill
products: Private-label bath tissue, paper towels
capacity_notes: Similar scale to Circleville. Opened 2020.
notes: Near Tulsa. Built after Circleville success. TAD technology.

name: Sofidel Lewiston Mill (fka Clearwater Paper)
products: Conventional tissue — private label
capacity_notes: 3 conventional tissue machines (1960s, 1970s, 1990s vintage). Sofidel's highest-volume US site. Acquired from Clearwater Paper 2024.
notes: Acquired from Clearwater Paper 2024. Buys pulp, steam, water from adjacent Clearwater Paper pulp mill. Converting investment planned. 3 machines — conventional tissue (not TAD).

name: Sofidel Haines City Plant
products: Private-label tissue/towel converting
capacity_notes: Converting facility. Part of original US entry (2012 FL acquisition).
notes: Part of Sofidel's 2012 US market entry via FL acquisition. 14 production sites across 11 US states total.

name: Edgewell Personal Care — Dover Plant
products: Carefree, Stayfree, Playtex feminine hygiene products
capacity_notes: Feminine hygiene converting. Former J&J brands.
panjiva_consignee_names: EDGEWELL PERSONAL CARE, ENERGIZER PERSONAL CARE
notes: Carefree/Stayfree/Playtex brands formerly J&J, now Edgewell (spun off from Energizer Holdings). Agreed to sell feminine care to Essity Nov 2025 ($340M). Fluff pulp consumer.
source: K-C history; Essity press release Nov 2025

name: Drylock Technologies — Eau Claire
products: Private-label baby diapers, adult incontinence products
capacity_notes: European diaper maker with US facility. High-speed converting.
notes: Belgian company. Private-label diaper/incontinence. Fluff pulp consumer. US market entry.

name: Domtar / Paper Excellence — Jesup Mill
products: Fluff pulp, market pulp
capacity_notes: ~350kt. Former Rayonier then acquired through Domtar/Paper Excellence chain.
notes: Altamaha River. Fluff pulp for hygiene products. Near Port of Savannah for export.

name: Domtar / Paper Excellence — Plymouth Mill
products: Fluff pulp, southern bleached softwood kraft
capacity_notes: ~300kt. Historic mill. Roanoke River access.
notes: Roanoke River. Former Weyerhaeuser → Domtar → Paper Excellence.
